author: Augustine of Hippo
book: A Treatise Concerning Man’s Perfection in Righteousness.

classification.cautionReason: No major mainstream heresy warning is attached by the preview classifier.

lineage: A Treatise Concerning Man’s Perfection in Righteousness.
The Third Passage. It is One Thing to Depart, and Another Thing to Have Departed, from All Sin. ‘There is None that Doeth Good,’—Of Whom This is to Be Understood.
title: The Third Passage. It is One Thing to Depart, and Another Thing to Have Departed, from All Sin. ‘There is None that Doeth Good,’—Of Whom This is to Be Understood.

verses.book: A Treatise Concerning Man’s Perfection in Righteousness.
verses.text: Chapter XIII.—(31.)

verses.book: A Treatise Concerning Man’s Perfection in Righteousness.
verses.text: The Third Passage.

verses.book: A Treatise Concerning Man’s Perfection in Righteousness.
verses.text: It is One Thing to Depart, and Another Thing to Have Departed, from All Sin.

verses.book: A Treatise Concerning Man’s Perfection in Righteousness.
verses.text: “There is None that Doeth Good,”—Of Whom This is to Be Understood.

verses.book: A Treatise Concerning Man’s Perfection in Righteousness.
verses.text: He has likewise propounded another question, as we shall proceed to show, but has failed to solve it; nay, he has rather rendered it more difficult, by first stating the testimony that had been quoted against him: “There is none that doeth good, no, not one;” and then resorting to seemingly contrary passages to show that there are persons who do good.

verses.book: A Treatise Concerning Man’s Perfection in Righteousness.
verses.text: This he succeeded, no doubt, in doing.

verses.book: A Treatise Concerning Man’s Perfection in Righteousness.
verses.text: It is, however, one thing for a man not to do good, and another thing not to be without sin, although he at the same time may do many good things.

verses.book: A Treatise Concerning Man’s Perfection in Righteousness.
verses.text: The passages, therefore, which he adduces are not really contrary to the statement that no person is without sin in this life.

verses.book: A Treatise Concerning Man’s Perfection in Righteousness.
verses.text: He does not, for his own part, explain in what sense it is declared that “there is none that doeth good, no, not one.”

verses.book: A Treatise Concerning Man’s Perfection in Righteousness.
verses.text: These are his words: “Holy David indeed says, ‘Hope thou in the Lord and be doing good.’”

verses.book: A Treatise Concerning Man’s Perfection in Righteousness.
verses.text: But this is a precept, and not an accomplished fact; and such a precept as is never kept by those of whom it is said, “There is none that doeth good, no, not one.”

verses.book: A Treatise Concerning Man’s Perfection in Righteousness.
verses.text: He adds: “Holy Tobit also said, ‘Fear not, my son, that we have to endure poverty; we shall have many blessings if we fear God, and depart from all sin, and do that which is good.’”

verses.book: A Treatise Concerning Man’s Perfection in Righteousness.
verses.text: Most true indeed it is, that man shall have many blessings when he shall have departed from all sin.

verses.book: A Treatise Concerning Man’s Perfection in Righteousness.
verses.text: Then no evil shall betide him; nor shall he have need of the prayer, “Deliver us from evil.”

verses.book: A Treatise Concerning Man’s Perfection in Righteousness.
verses.text: Although even now every man who progresses, advancing ever with an upright purpose, departs from all sin, and becomes further removed from it as he approaches nearer to the fulness and perfection of the righteous state; because even concupiscence itself, which is sin dwelling in our flesh, never ceases to diminish in those who are making progress, although it still remains in their mortal members.

verses.book: A Treatise Concerning Man’s Perfection in Righteousness.
verses.text: It is one thing, therefore, to depart from all sin,—a process which is even now in operation,—and another thing to have departed from all sin, which shall happen in the state of future perfection.

verses.book: A Treatise Concerning Man’s Perfection in Righteousness.
verses.text: But still, even he who has departed already from evil, and is continuing to do so, must be allowed to be a doer of good.

verses.book: A Treatise Concerning Man’s Perfection in Righteousness.
verses.text: How then is it said, in the passage which he has quoted and left unsolved, “There is none that doeth good, no, not one,” unless that the Psalmist there censures some one nation, amongst whom there was not a man that did good, wishing to remain “children of men,” and not sons of God, by whose grace man becomes good, in order to do good?

verses.book: A Treatise Concerning Man’s Perfection in Righteousness.
verses.text: For we must suppose the Psalmist here to mean that “good” which he describes in the context, saying, “God looked down from heaven upon the children of men, to see if there were any that did understand, and seek God.”

verses.book: A Treatise Concerning Man’s Perfection in Righteousness.
verses.text: Such good then as this, seeking after God, there was not a man found who pursued it, no, not one; but this was in that class of men which is predestinated to destruction.

verses.book: A Treatise Concerning Man’s Perfection in Righteousness.
verses.text: It was upon such that God looked down in His foreknowledge, and passed sentence.
